intro CKnight
  have h₁ : C ∈ Knight := by assumption
  have h₂ : C = B := by
    -- Since Knight = {B} or {C}, and C ∈ Knight, then C must be B if Knight = {B}
    -- If Knight = {C}, then C = C, but we have B ∈ Knight, so B = C
    have h₃ : Knight = {B} ∨ Knight = {C} := by tauto
    cases h₃ with
    | inl h₃ =>
      rw [h₃] at h₁
      simp only [Finset.mem_singleton] at h₁
      exact h₁.symm
    | inr h₃ =>
      rw [h₃] at h₁
      simp only [Finset.mem_singleton] at h₁
      <;> simp_all
      <;> aesop
  simp_all [Finset.mem_singleton]
  <;> aesop

exact BneC CKnight.symm
      contradiction
    exact h₁
  ⟩

intro CKnight
    -- If C is a knight, then Knight = {C}
    have h1 : Knight = {C} := by
      apply Finset.Subset.antisymm
      · -- Prove that Knight ⊆ {C}
        have h2 : ∀ (x : Inhabitant), x ∈ Knight → x ∈ ({C} : Finset Inhabitant) := by
          intro x hx
          have hx' : x = A ∨ x = B ∨ x = C := all x
          cases hx' with
          | inl hxA =>
            -- If x = A, then A ∈ Knight, but A ∉ Knight
            exfalso
            have hA' : A ∈ Knight := by
              rw [hxA] at hx
              exact hx
            have hA'' : A ∉ Knight := by
              tauto
            tauto
          | inr hx' =>
            cases hx' with
            | inl hxB =>
              -- If x = B, then B ∈ Knight, but if Knight = {C}, this is a contradiction
              exfalso
              have hB' : B ∈ Knight := by
                rw [hxB] at hx
                exact hx
              have hB'' : B ∈ Knight := by assumption
              have h2 : Knight = {C} := by tauto
              rw [h2] at hB''
              simp [Finset.mem_singleton] at hB''
              <;> tauto
            | inr hxC =>
              -- If x = C, then x ∈ {C}
              rw [hxC]
              <;> simp
        exact Finset.subset_iff.mpr h2
      · -- Prove that {C} ⊆ Knight
        have h2 : C ∈ Knight := CKnight
        simp_all [Finset.subset_iff, Finset.mem_singleton]
        <;> aesop
    -- Since Knight = {C}, we have a contradiction with notallknave
    have h2 : Knave = {A, B, C} := by
      have h3 : A ∈ Knave := AKnave
      have h4 : B ∈ Knave := by
        set_knight_to_knave
        <;> simp_all [stB]
        <;> tauto
      have h5 : C ∈ Knave := by
        tauto
      have h6 : ∀ (x : Inhabitant), x = A ∨ x = B ∨ x = C := all
      exact (everyone_in_set_eq all).mpr ⟨h3, h4, h5⟩
    tauto
  exact hC
